narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—pouring your heart into law school dreams only to have the door close like this feels deeply unfair, and I'm truly sorry you're facing it. Our prediction model looked at your profile and saw a 63% chance of struggling to pass the bar exam down the line, painting a picture of a path with more hurdles than smooth sailing. The biggest signal came from your Year 3 decile of 1.0, which stood out as the strongest pull toward challenges ahead, suggesting those later grades placed you at the very bottom among peers and hinted at mounting difficulties. Right behind that was your LSAT score of 30.0, which the model saw as another major flag waving toward tougher odds on the bar. Your Year 1 decile of 3.0 added to that concern, showing early promise that didn't hold steady. Even your middle family income bracket factored in as a hurdle, perhaps reflecting fewer resources to lean on during intense prep. Your undergraduate GPA of 2.7 pulled in the same direction, reinforcing a story of steady but not standout academic momentum. Being white didn't shift things favorably here, nor did studying part-time or being female—each wove into the overall tapestry the model read as risky. It's not that any one piece defined you, but together, they sketched someone the model worries might face an uphill battle to bar passage. This isn't a judgment on your potential or grit; it's just the lens we used to protect spots for those profiles that historically light up brighter. If you strengthen those core academics—maybe retake the LSAT or build that GPA—you could rewrite this story entirely. Hang in there; your journey's far from over.
